I think the most brilliant thing about the Lois Lane/Superman interview is the fact that these kinds of politics in a relationship happen and the arguments happen just like this. Just watched the movie. I went in very very skeptical. I came out LOVING this movie. James Gunn has blown me away! He made a true Comic Book accurate movie and already beautifully set up a universe. He blew my mind with how many things he nailed. How did he get me to enjoy Krypto? How did he make Supes to work in a modern context? But I promise you, he did it! I can actually have genuine hope for the DC franchise from here on out! But, can he nail a second film? Time will tell. But a tip of the hat to him! Genuinely filled with childlike happiness 🙂

You can point me to 1000 monkeys making youtube videos praising this film but unless this movie goes over $700 Million, Warner wont make a profit which begs the question "why make continue down this road with Gunn if he can't make a profit for the company?" NUMBERS DO NOT LIE.

@JhoanSebastianTangarifeMoreno THE BOX OFFICE RETURN, THAT'S WHO! Unless this movie makes $600 Million there will be no point on continuing with a franchise that doesn't make a profit. NUMBERS DON"T LIE.

@JhoanSebastianTangarifeMoreno Just to help you understand Deadpool 3 earned $438.3 million worldwide for the initial weekend while James Gunn's Superman grossed $217 million. Negating this gap between both movies, the gross earning of a movie is not the total net earnings for a studio. You have to take into account all the local distributors, local marketing firms and movie houses worldwide so in the end you can say that a movie that earns $1 Billion  will only have a net earning of $500 to $600 million , not counting production costs, so UNLESS James Gunn's Superman gets a huge up tick in sales instead of a drop off, I'm afraid this movie will barely break even so no matter if all your friends love this movie the reality is not enough people spent money on it. Just saying.....

I saw Superman in 1978 when I was 11 years old and I thought it was the greatest thing ever. Also the first Toby McGuire Spiderman many years later was pretty cool. Then I saw like 4, 5 more and they were ok. Afer that I went to about 20 more, with my kids but I slept though most of them. So boring. I've been done with superhero movies for a long time now. I saw this movie in IMAX I loved it for several reasons.

This is the first time Superman has felt like he is part of the world he is protecting.

For me Chris Reeves is still the gold standard for Superman because of the generation I grew up in. But This is the first time I've seen Superman portrayed as the Big Blue Boy Scout the way he is sometimes depicted in the animated series.

Erica Durance still holds the title for the hottest Lois lane. But this is the first time that Superman and Lois Lane have had genuine chemistry it in a feature film.

This is the first time where Superman has been strong enough to be visually impressive and vulnerable enough to go on an interesting adventure.

This is the first time in a movie that Lois Lane has been Interesting and likeable and a real part of the story and not just a damsel in distress. She had real agency in the movie and help drive the plot forward without falling into the trope of being the quote unquote strong independent woman.

I love the fact that all the side characters played such a huge part in this movie without overshadowing the lead I loved it.

Movie Review: Superman (2025) — The Man of Steel Returns, and So Do Your Goosebumps (way too many times!❤️‍🔥

Let me preface this by saying: bring a jacket, because you will catch a chill. Not from the theatre AC, but from the sheer number of goosebumps this movie triggers every two minutes. Honestly, my arm hair started a union halfway through the film.

A Nostalgia-Packed, Heartfelt Rebirth

Superman (2025) isn’t just a reboot — it’s a love letter. A love letter to the mythos, to the fans, and yes, to every version of the character who has ever donned the cape. The film walks the impossible tightrope between bold reinvention and deep reverence. And it flies.

Without revealing any spoilers (because I, too, value my life), let's just say that the opening moments will have longtime fans leaning forward with a suspicious glint in their eye, only to whisper, "No way... they did that." Yes, yes they did. And more.

There are cameos so perfectly timed, so gloriously handled, that my inner child jumped out of my seat — and briefly tried to apply for citizenship in Krypton. From subtle nods to jaw-dropping appearances, the film reads like a greatest hits compilation that somehow doesn’t feel like fan service — it feels earned. Also there was a guy wearing a golden helmet in the movie but apparently people couldn't see him🤷🏻...nobody knows why🤷🏻


The new Superman brings something both fresh and achingly familiar. There’s a vulnerability to him — an awareness that even gods carry the weight of the world on their shoulders. The emotional arc? Poetic. The action sequences? Punchy, kinetic, but grounded in story. There’s a fight in the second act (you’ll know when you see it) that had me vibrating in my seat like a tuning fork made of emotion. One thing I'd say was extra or maybe just a little odd was the "Relationship" aspect of Superman , i respect them showing a more Human side of him but Lois being a problematic type partner was a bit odd....still they figured it out so it's all good in the end👌🏻

And the score. THE SCORE. I counted six distinct moments where my spine transformed into a xylophone of chills. You know it’s good when you start looking around the theatre to see if anyone else is crying over sound design.

By Minute 3: Goosebumps!

Minute 30: Goosebumps on top of goosebumps. Double-decker style!!

Minute 60: I had to pause (mentally) and ask myself, “Do I even have enough skin left to keep producing chills?”

Final act: My hair follicles achieved enlightenment!!!

The Cast Shines Like Kryptonian Crystals

The ensemble delivers. Without naming names, because again — no spoilers — certain actors return in ways that had the audience collectively gasp like a synchronized cult of comic-book nerds. Others make their DC Universe debut with such charisma, you’d think they were born in a cape.

Special props to the supporting roles, too — even those with brief screen time manage to add something meaningful. And props to adding a Brown guy who always believes and supports the main hero when nobody else does (extra brown guy approval points).

Humor, Humanity, and Hope

What truly elevates Superman (2025) is its emotional intelligence. It’s funny without being quippy, dramatic without being melodramatic, and hopeful without feeling naïve. It's the kind of film where, even when your jaw isn’t dropping, your heart is.

And it’s the most Superman thing in the world to end on a note that makes you believe in goodness again. I walked out of the theatre taller, like gravity had lightened a little. Probably because the goosebumps were holding me up.

Final Verdict❤️‍🔥

This isn’t just another superhero movie — it’s the superhero movie. A cinematic sunbeam cutting through the multiverse smog. DC has re-forged the symbol of hope, and it's brighter than ever.

Score: 9.8/10 (.2 deducted only because my poor arms are permanently textured now.)

I feel this film version has the potential to do something right by truly exploring Superman’s humanity and that would be a major win. Unlike Man of Steel, which focused more on his immense power and intergalactic battles, it didn’t delve as deeply into his internal character. While he restrained himself to avoid destruction and killing, it still painted him more as a cosmic force than a nuanced figure. That restraint, though, is exactly what should define him not just his power, but the choices he makes because of his humanity. He needs to be remembered not only as a savior, but also as someone who challenges the status quo.

Dawn of Justice as much as it was not a direct hit" and The Snyder Cut of Justice League felt like a step in the right direction. Superman's personality came through more as the story progressed, showing both strength and emotional depth. It's unfortunate Snyder didn’t get to complete his full vision for the DC universe. Still, this upcoming film might surprise us we’ll just have to wait and see.

What Superman is this going to be?

This: I'm not doing what I am doing for a country. I'm not doing it for any side. I'm not doing it to be a savior, a king, or a ruler. I don't want to rule over anyone or a place. I see someone in trouble, I'm going to help. I see a disaster about to unfold, I'm going to go and stop it. If a war is brewing, I am going to try and end it before it begins without loss. Anything or anyone that tries to end all humanity, I'm going to prevent it/them. I'm not one to be feared. I'm not one to be worshipped. I just want to be a symbol of hope, of kindness. The good of what humanity can bring to everyone. No prejudice, no hate, no ambition of power and control. Just the ability everyone possesses inside them to be and do good for everyone.

This movies look really good. It explores a question I've had a long time ago about supe's behavior during the Dark Knight Returns movie part 2. In that movie, Superman works for the government and diligently follows their orders, while Bruce works for himself. Thus creating an ideological dispute between the two. 

Based on this trailer, it seems like Supes is saving people and taking action on his own accord. basically living a structureless (lack of government authority) superhero lifestyle; essentially, vigilantism like Batman. It seems like he'll struggle to come to terms with the fact that there are rules and regulations to this world that must be followed. That things aren't as black and white as "these two countries are at war so let me stop them from fighting each other, and that's it."

Somebody as powerful as supes can't be out there flying doing whatever he wants, because it'll make the public uneasy and insecure about what he stands for. But if he were to frame himself as an ambassador of the government, working in tangent with them to fulfil their mission statement, then the people and government officials will have more trust in him. Either fall in line with the rules of society or be labelled a criminal.

I saw the original Christopher Reeve Superman in theaters. I'm happy to see a return to themes of hope and goodness (not to mention a properly blue and red costume), but can we tone down the millennial snark and goofiness a bit? That was never a part of what made the early films good and I don't see it going over well here. Also, I really can't see Superman getting that bent out of shape over being questioned over his actions. That's part of what makes him Superman; he's just not that emotionally fragile. That's why it hits hard when he does get emotional; it takes a lot to get him there.
This is a movie. A movie creates conflicts and resolutions. If you’re not used to that by now, then don’t watch a movie.

For years, the media has gazed upward, chasing the ‘Super.’ But now, we’re invited closer, to see the ‘Man.’
He's no longer just a force of nature, an unreacheable god in the clouds. He falters, he feels. He loves, he fears. He has a Ma, a Pa and a loyal dog at his side. 
He has a heart that breaks. A soul that hopes. And in that, he becomes more than myth. He becomes known.
To make him carry the weight of the human experiences and tribulations does not make him weaker. It makes him real. And maybe, even stronger.

Lois Lane, like many reporters, is nothing more than a Monday Morning Quarterback who benefits from hindsight that isn't available in real time. You have to act in times of crisis. You don't have the luxury of knowing every possible outcome of your actions. Especially, if you're Superman. He is one of one. It is made even more distasteful when the people who benefit from your actions act as if since you didn't bat 1.000 in your actions, you should never have taken a swing. And if he had never taken a swing and did nothing, he would have received criticism for that as well. As Colonel Jessup said, in A Few Good Men, "I have neither the time nor the inclination to explain myself to a [wo]man who rises and sleeps under the blanket of the very freedom that I provide, and then questions the manner in which I provide it!"

I am super excited about this superhero film. I can appreciate Gunn because he is willing to make a superhero movie and doesn't have any shame in that. Too many franchises try to "elevate" themselves above their comic ancestors by making the suits super realistic and grounded to the detriment of the suspension of belief. Like sure Batman might have more armor, but that is because Batman is a mortal fighting in close combat, and is using the shadows as a weapon. Of course his armor reflects that fact. It would be weird if it didn't/ Superman on the other hand has his arsenal built in to the form, so can work a lot harder on being recognized and branding as Superman, defender of the weak. 

It is also refreshing to see a true paragon ideology once in a while. We have seen 3 dozen morally grey Supermanalikes wandering around, it is about time we had a real deal once in a while, you know?

But that's what makes him boring as a character, to me at least. If he's already perfect, then there's is no real character to Superman

That perfect take is the laziest misunderstanding of Superman ever because he’s not boring because he’s powerful, he’s compelling because of how he chooses to use that power. Restraint, morality, hope. Those are harder to maintain than a dark, brooding spiral. If you think perfection is boring, maybe you’ve only been watching characters who punch their problems, not those who rise above them.

Superman and his villains reflect the choice of how you ultimately use your power.

Superman knows that with all his power, he should try and do what he can, to help the people of the world because he cares and is one of them.

Luthor, on the other hand, is in the top 3 smartest people in the world, massively influential with limitless assets at his fingertips. But he has only ever chosen to be selfish.

Superman is about the choice to be selfless and his journey to inspire every being of Earth to make that same selfless choice, and it can be a hard choice, because it is always easier to fall into selfishness.

This is a younger Superman. Not the rushed Henry cavil Superman, but one right in the middle of the world fearing him. He is still learning how to be that confidant collected hero. But being human is a big selling point here. If all we see is a cutout poster child of what we know Superman TYPICALLY is, then there’s no room to grow and anchor us as an audience to HIS growth. This will be the most comic accurate long form narrative of Superman and the eventual justice league. We have to get there. And hopefully not do what they did last time and rush there. Because we all saw how that ended up.
